topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The discovery was accidental, a misplaced bookmark slipped between pages of a thick, leather-bound journal. My hands trembled as I flipped through the script. Every chapter, every meticulously detailed description of my morning routine, my awkward silences, the way I chewed my lip when concentrating—it was all there, rendered in prose so intimate, so painfully accurate, that it felt like an invasion. A cold, heavy pressure settled in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I quickly slammed the book shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet apartment. I stood there, staring at the closed cover, my gaze darting around the room as if expecting someone to materialize and expose the secret. When my partner finally walked in, their smile was bright, oblivious. "Rough day?" they asked, their tone light, entirely missing the seismic shift that had just occurred within me. I mumbled something incoherent, my throat suddenly tight, and retreated to the bedroom, pulling the covers up to my chin, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked inside a hollowed-out copy of *Wuthering Heights*. The handwriting was undeniably his—elegant, flowing, utterly consuming. Reading the passages detailing my smallest anxieties, the precise shade of blue I preferred, the way I reacted to sudden noises—it was like holding a mirror up to a hidden, vulnerable self. A flush crept up my neck, hot and undeniable. I felt a desperate urge to burn the pages, to erase the evidence of this profound exposure. When he came home later, I tried to act normal, pouring myself a glass of water, but my movements were jerky, my eyes fixed on the floor. "What are you looking at?" he inquired, concern softening his features. I quickly slid the book under a stack of magazines, my knuckles white where I gripped the glass. The silence stretched, thick and suffocating, as I fought the urge to confess the horrifying reality of what I had just uncovered.

[story 3]

It started subtly, a misplaced note, then the full realization when I found the manuscript hidden beneath his desk. The words painted a picture of my entire existence, dissected and immortalized in his careful narrative. A wave of profound discomfort washed over me, leaving a strange, hollow ache behind my ribs. I kept glancing at the desk, the paper shimmering under the lamplight. Every time he moved, I braced myself, anticipating a reaction, a question, a moment where the truth might spill out. When he finally noticed my distraction, he leaned over, his expression shifting from casual to curious. "Found something interesting?" he murmured, his voice laced with a gentle inquiry. I shook my head quickly, unable to meet his eyes. My cheeks felt strangely numb, and I focused intensely on a dust mote dancing in the beam of light, trying desperately to anchor myself to something real, anything other than the suffocating weight of his literary gaze.
